I once watched a senior developer explain signals for forty minutes and confuse an entire room.
The next day, a junior explained the same thing in four sentences. Everyone got it.
That bothered me for weeks.
Because if four sentences are enough, why do most articles need four thousand words? The answer is simple. Most articles explain the API. Almost nobody explains the mental model.
Today you get the mental model. Four sentences. Everything after that is proof, code, and the traps I want you to avoid.
Read the four sentences slowly. Then let me show you why each one changes how you write Angular.
The Four Sentences

• A signal is a value that knows who is reading it.

• A computed is a value that knows what it depends on.

• An effect is code that runs when its dependencies change.

• Change detection now updates only the places where a signal was read.
That is the whole model. Everything else in the signals API is a convenience built on top of these four ideas.
Let me prove each one to you.
Sentence 1: A Signal Knows Who Is…
